meta_title: For Students: content creation to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to content creation and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Content Creation to Solve 'No Project Ideas'
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' trap by building content creation tools. Learn how to use Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first portfolio piece.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the perfect idea. Build a content creation engine that automates blog generation or social media scheduling. By solving your own workflow problems using Antigravity and Claude Code, you create a high-value portfolio project that demonstrates real-world engineering skills and AI integration expertise.
content.intro:
The blank editor is the graveyard of engineering ambition. You sit down, fingers poised over the keyboard, only to be paralyzed by the silence of a missing project idea. This 'blank slate' syndrome kills more careers than any difficult algorithm ever could. You aren't lacking talent; you are lacking a sandbox.

Today, we pivot. We move from passive consumption to active creation. By building a content generation tool, you aren't just writing code—you are building a machine that produces output. It is time to stop looking for ideas and start building the infrastructure that makes them obsolete.

steps.title: Define the Content Schema
steps.description: Establish the data structure for your content. We define the 'what' before we build the 'how'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript interface for a BlogPost object including title, meta description, markdown body, and tags. Include a validation schema using Zod.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create types/content.ts based on the generated Zod schema'

steps.title: Initialize the Agent Engine
steps.description: Set up the core orchestration layer to connect your inputs to the LLM.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an orchestrator class that takes a topic string and returns a structured JSON object containing a blog outline and a 500-word draft.

steps.title: Implement the Drafting Loop
steps.description: Automate the iterative drafting process to ensure high-quality, consistent output.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a recursive function that takes a draft, evaluates it against a style guide, and applies one iteration of improvements.

steps.title: Connect the File System
steps.description: Bridge the gap between AI generation and your local workspace.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a file utility that saves the generated JSON content into a timestamped markdown file in the /content folder.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create src/utils/fileWriter.ts to handle local markdown export'

steps.title: Deploy the CLI Interface
steps.description: Wrap your tool in a professional command-line interface for easy interaction.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a CLI command using Commander.js that accepts a --topic flag and triggers the drafting engine.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npm install commander && node src/cli.ts --topic "AI Trends"'

pro_tips: Never hardcode your API keys; use a local .env file and add it to .gitignore immediately.
Implement 'Temperature' controls in your prompt to allow for creative vs. analytical output modes.
Always include a 'human-in-the-loop' step where the AI output is displayed for approval before file write.
Log your token usage in a local JSON file to track the cost of your development cycle.
Build a 'dry-run' mode that simulates the API call without spending credits during testing.
content.next_steps: Once your CLI tool is operational, wrap it in a GitHub repository, document the setup in a README.md, and share your first generated article on LinkedIn to start building your professional brand.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the workflow to turn any topic into a production-ready application. Stop waiting for inspiration and start shipping code that works. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
